findings.description: PDF page 1 footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management...') is rendered as inline body content in the HTML between the report outline (Section I–IV list) and the start of Section I. History/Background, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the footer is at the bottom of page 1 and is visually separate from the body. In the HTML, the same footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management / 1646 Mail Service Center | 217 West Jones Street | Raleigh, 27699-1646 / 919 707 8200') appears inline between the outline and the History/Background heading, as if it were part of the document body.

findings.description: The PDF page-1 footer (agency address and phone number) is inserted mid-content in the HTML body, between the introductory outline and Section I, breaking reading flow. It appears inside a <footer> element but is placed between body paragraphs rather than at the end of the document.
findings.evidence:
HTML has '

State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Waste Management
1646 Mail Service Center | 217 West Jones Street | Raleigh, 27699-1646
919 707 8200

' placed between the outline (ol type="I") and '

I. History/Background

'. In the PDF, this only appears as a page footer at the bottom of page 1.
